text:
In the heart of a bustling city lived a young rabbit named Thimble. She was small and quick, known for her adventurous spirit. One fine spring morning, Thimble woke up with an itch in her nose that was becoming unbearable. She thought if she could just find the perfect itch-relief scratching post, she'd be all set.

After a breakfast of hoppy cereal and a spring water, Thimble bounded down the street, her fluffy tail bouncing. After a few missteps, she found herself on a path less traveled, the shade of willows whispering secrets. As she poked her nose beneath the gnarled roots, her nose began to swell.

She hopped back, lamenting the harshness of nature.

"Oh, bother," Thimble sighed. "It seems even nature bites."
